Title: The coexpression of two desaturases provides an optimized reduction of saturates in camelina oil

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: saturated fatty acids in seed oil (especially palmitic acid, 16:0); increased monounsaturated fatty acids including 16:1
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: seed oil fatty acid desaturation / triacylglycerol biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream branch point reducing saturated acyl-CoA pool before incorporation into storage lipids
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly reduces saturated fatty acid pool and increases monounsaturates in seed oil
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: high transgene expression in developing seeds
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in camelina; qPCR of developing seeds; gas chromatography fatty acid profiling; oil content measurement; germination and seedling establishment assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces seed oil saturates moderately without compromising oil content, germination, or seedling establishment
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Seed-specific FAT5 expression in camelina increased 16:1 and reduced 16:0. 2. In homozygous FAT5-11 seeds, 16:0 fell to about 4.4% and total saturates to 8.6% versus 12.9% in control. 3. FAT5 expression did not significantly reduce oil content or impair germination/seedling establishment. 4. FAT5 alone was less effective in camelina than previously observed in Arabidopsis.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth chamber, 22°C, continuous illumination; seed-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: lower saturates improve biofuel cold-flow properties; increased monounsaturates improve fuel and edible oil quality
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: useful transgene for lowering seed oil saturates with minimal fitness cost; particularly valuable when stacked with DES9* and potentially with FAD2 suppression to optimize biofuel oil composition
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: alone provides only moderate reduction of saturates in camelina, less effective than in Arabidopsis
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: In coexpression line FAT5/DES9*-7, FAT5 transcript level was less than one tenth of FAT5-11 yet contributed to strong saturate reduction and phenotype rescue.

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: saturated fatty acids in seed oil (especially palmitic acid, 16:0); increased monounsaturated fatty acids
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: seed oil fatty acid desaturation / glycerolipid desaturation pathway
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: midstream branch acting on membrane glycerolipids that feed storage oil composition
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: monounsaturated glycerolipid fatty acyl chains including 16:1-containing species
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: desaturates 16:0/18:0 fatty acyl chains on glycerolipids to corresponding monounsaturated products
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly lowers saturated fatty acids in seed oil by desaturating glycerolipid-bound saturates
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: expressed in developing seeds; lower in coexpression line than in DES9*-14 single line
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: seed-specific transgenic expression in camelina; qPCR; gas chromatography fatty acid profiling; oil content measurement; germination and seedling establishment assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: strongly reduces seed oil saturates but compromises seed physiology, reducing oil content and seedling performance when expressed alone
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DES9* alone reduced 16:0 strongly in camelina, with total saturates lowered to about 4.6%. 2. DES9*-14 seeds had about 20% lower oil content than control. 3. DES9* expression alone caused poor germination, impaired seedling establishment, and reduced fresh weight. 4. When coexpressed with FAT5, DES9* contributed to very low saturates without the severe seed defects seen in single-gene lines.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: growth chamber, 22°C, continuous illumination; seed-specific transgene expression
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: lower saturates improve biofuel cold-flow properties; higher monounsaturates improve fuel and edible oil quality
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: membrane fatty acid/glycerolipid desaturase; engineered variant with E69R/I129A/G214R substitutions
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: powerful saturate-lowering enzyme but best deployed in combination with FAT5 to avoid oil-content and germination penalties
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: expression alone decreases oil content and severely compromises germination and establishment
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DES9* was generated by directed evolution in yeast from a cyanobacterial enzyme and contains E69R, I129A, and G214R substitutions relative to the parental protein.
